mnemonics.title: EPR Key Elements
mnemonics.content:
P - Producer responsibility
R - Recycling targets
C - Collection mechanisms
A - Authorization from CPCB
C - Citizens must cooperate

mnemonics.title: Hazardous Substances记忆
mnemonics.content:
Lead (Pb), Mercury (Hg), Cadmium (Cd), Chromium (Cr6), Brominated Flame Retardants (BFR)
记忆: Pee Kaat Ke Cr Bees Raha (पीकात के क्र बीस रहा)

mnemonics.title: Stakeholders记忆
mnemonics.content:
CPCB - Central Control
SPCB - State Control
PRO - Producer Representative
RECYCLER - Material Recoverer
CONSUMER - Final Contributor

acronyms.usage: Holds electronics manufacturers accountable for end-of-life product management
Entity that helps producers fulfill their e-waste collection targets
National regulatory body for e-waste management in India
State-level implementation and monitoring of e-waste rules
EU directive restricting hazardous substances in electronics

visualTips.visualization: Draw a cycle: Producer → Products sold → Consumer uses → Consumer disposes → Collection center → Dismantler → Recycler → Raw materials back to Producer
Use a rising bar chart: Year 1: 60%, Year 2: 70%, Year 3: 80%
Pyramid diagram: CPCB (top/regulator) → SPCB (state implementation) → PRO (producer support) → Recycler/Dismantler (operators) → Consumer (generator)

analogies.analogy: Like a restaurant being responsible for collecting and disposing of its takeaway containers, not just making and selling them
Like a real estate agent helping homeowners sell their property - PRO helps producers manage their e-waste burden
Like a stock market but for e-waste - transparent pricing and trading between sellers and buyers
Like a sales target for a company - producers must meet specific collection percentages each year
